items.title: EPRI Journal: Celebrating Fifty Years of the Long View - EPRI Journal
items.content_text: Share this article:By Chris Warren EPRI had a very clear mission when it was founded in 1972. items.title: Restarting Nuclear Power Plants: Preserving and Restoring Value - EPRI Journal
items.content_text: Share this article:Recent growth in electricity demand, expanding carbon-free energy goals, and renewed concerns about energy security are driving utilities to reconsider nuclear power plants that have been permanently shut down or are approaching retirement. EPRI examines the technical, regulatory, and business factors that influence whether these facilities can return to service and what steps [вҖҰ]

items.title: Why Grid Models Need a Solid Data Foundation - EPRI Journal
items.content_text: Share this article:By Chris Warren The distribution grid isnвҖҷt what it used to be. Traditionally, the lower-voltage grid carried electricity in one direction from large power plants to businesses and residents who needed it. Though distribution grids have always been inherently complex, the one-way flow of electrons from power plants and the transmission grid to [вҖҰ]

items.title: EPRI's Roadmap for a Rapidly Growing Battery Storage Industry - EPRI Journal
items.content_text: Share this article:By Chris Warren Installations of battery energy storage are rapidly accelerating across the globe. According to the International Energy Agency (IEA), 108 GW of new battery storage was deployed globally in 2025, an increase of 40% over 2024 and 11 times more than in 2021. A similar story is playing out in the [вҖҰ]

items.title: The Long Wait - EPRI Journal
items.content_text: Share this article:How EPRI helped JapanвҖҷs nuclear industry navigate an extended shutdown, modernize for the future, and generate valuable lessons for the rest of the world The second in a series of three articles about EPRIвҖҷs long-term commitment to the Japanese nuclear industry post-Fukushima examines the days and weeks after the 2011 tsunami. items.title: The Electric Airport - EPRI Journal
items.content_text: Share this article:A new EPRI offering draws on decades of experience and wide-ranging expertise to help airports prepare for an electric liftoff. By Chris Warren Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is a city within a city. The airport covers 5.5 square miles and regularly serves well over 70 million passengers annually, making it one of [вҖҰ]

items.title: Fire Safety Considerations for Electric Vehicles and Charging Stations - EPRI Journal
items.content_text: Share this article:What Fleet Managers Need to Know As electric vehicles (EVs) become an increasingly common part of commercial and utility fleets, questions around fire safety arise. items.title: Beyond Billing: How Utilities Can Unlock the Full Value of Advanced Metering Infrastructure - EPRI Journal
items.content_text: Share this article:By Chris Warren Judging by the numbers alone, the rollout of advanced metering infrastructure (AMI) has been an undeniable success. Utilities such as Pacific Gas & Electric (PG&E) and Florida Power & Light (FP&L) were among the first to launch ambitious smart meter deployments in the mid- to late 2000s. It was the [вҖҰ]

items.title: Synthetic Customer Load Data: Approaches, Limitations, and the Role of Generative AI - EPRI Journal
items.content_text: Share this article:Electric utilities are navigating a period of rapid change driven by electrification, renewable energy integration, climate variability, and evolving customer behavior. These trends are placing new demands on planning processes that have historically relied on empirical load data, such as advanced metering infrastructure (AMI) records or curated reference datasets. items.title: Planning Process Education Recordings Available | PJM Inside Lines
items.content_text: Recordings are available from the July 16 special session of the Transmission Expansion Advisory Committee, a daylong educational meeting on PJMвҖҷs regional planning process.

items.title: PJM Board Approves Transmission Project Addition, Changes To Support Grid Reliability | PJM Inside Lines
items.content_text: The PJM Board of Managers has authorized an immediate-need reliability project and changes to existing projects in the Regional Transmission Expansion Plan (RTEP) to help provide the most efficient, economical and reliable supply of power for the 67 million people PJM serves across 13 states and the District of Columbia.

items.title: PJM Hot Weather Operations Update вҖ“ July 15, 2026 | PJM Inside Lines
items.content_text: Maximum Generation Alert Issued for July 16; Hot Weather Alert Continues Through July 17

items.title: PJM Capacity Auction Procures 138,318 MW of Generation Resources as Work Continues To Address Growing Electricity Demand | PJM Inside Lines
items.content_text: Price Comes in at FERC-Approved Cap, Down 2.5%; Total Capacity Cleared Is 6,831 MW Short of Reliability Requirement

items.title: PJM Hot Weather Operations Update вҖ“ July 14, 2026 | PJM Inside Lines
items.content_text: Maximum Generation Alert Issued for July 15; Hot Weather Alert Extended Through July 17

items.title: Hot Weather Alert Issued for July 14вҖ“17 | PJM Inside Lines
items.content_text: PJM has issued a Hot Weather Alert for the entire region PJM serves for July 14вҖ“17 ahead of expected 90-plus-degree weather.

items.title: PJM Serves Load Through Record-Breaking July Heat | PJM Inside Lines
items.content_text: PJM maintained reliable operations July 2 through a preliminary energy use record, requiring all available generation to keep the power flowing.

items.title: Day-Long Planning Process Education Session Set for July 16 | PJM Inside Lines
items.content_text: PJM is hosting a special session of the Transmission Expansion Advisory Committee on July 16 to provide an overview of the annual planning process for a general audience, including how PJM determines when new transmission is needed, how projects are selected and how the public can participate.

items.title: PJM Hot Weather Operations Update: July 3 | PJM Inside Lines
items.content_text: Hot Weather Alert Remains in Effect Through July 4 and into July 5 for Mid-Atlantic and Dominion Zones

items.title: PJM Hot Weather Operations Update вҖ“ July 2, 2026 | PJM Inside Lines
items.content_text: Maximum Generation Alert Issued for July 3; Hot Weather Alert Remains in Effect Through July 4
